get.summary: Fetch a list of Audit logs

parameters.name: x-kolide-api-version
parameters.description: Specify the API version to be used

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Specify the cursor to begin returning results from

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Specify the number of records to return in each response.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
A query clause used to filter the set of resources returned. Use
`:` for exact matches (including for boolean fields), `~` for
partial-string matches. `:["a","b","c"]` or `:[1,2,3]`
can be used for fields that support multi-value search.
 Datetime fields support exact-matching
(`:`) as well as less-than (`<`) and greater-than (`>`). The
searchable fields are:

`timestamp` , `actor_name` , `description`

Search clauses can be combined with the ` AND ` and ` OR ` operators.

For more information, see the [search documentation](https://www.kolide.com/docs/developers/api#search).

timestamp:.summary: search the 'timestamp' property of the Audit Log records using ':' operator

timestamp<.summary: search the 'timestamp' property of the Audit Log records using '<' operator

timestamp>.summary: search the 'timestamp' property of the Audit Log records using '>' operator

actor_name:.summary: search the 'actor_name' property of the Audit Log records using ':' operator

actor_name~.summary: search the 'actor_name' property of the Audit Log records using '~' operator

description:.summary: search the 'description' property of the Audit Log records using ':' operator

description~.summary: search the 'description' property of the Audit Log records using '~' operator

200.description: A paginated collection of Audit logs

data.description:
Kolide maintains an audit log of user actions in the Kolide web
console. Examples of tracked events include, but are not limited to:
  - Inviting & removing Kolide team members
  - Changing the display name of a device
  - Requesting a device deletion
  - updating a Check's blocking configuration
  - Approving/denying a registration or exemption request

next.description: The full URL that should be used to fetch the next page of results. This will be blank if there are no more results to fetch

next_cursor.description: the pagination cursor that can be used to fetch the page of results following the current page. This will be blank if there are no more results to fetch

count.description: the number of records in the current page of results

delete.description:
Remove an existing device registration. This will prevent the
registered owner from authenticating with the device. If this device
is the only registered device for the person, their
Trust-On-First-Use (TOFU) status will be reset, allowing them to
register another device without needing admin approval. Deleting a
mobile device's registration will also remove the device from the
Kolide.

delete.description:
Mark a device as pending deletion. Note that the deletion will not
happen immediately: the deletion can be canceled in the web
interface until the device is actually deleted. The Kolide agent
on the device will also be disabled to prevent re-enrollment.
If this device is the only registered device for a user, their
Trust-On-First-Use (TOFU) status will be reset, allowing them to
register another device without needing admin approval.
